must_detect.matched_text:
Internal Incident Report — Strictly Confidential — Inside Information under Part XIVA SFO — Not for selective disclosure.
Issuer: Harbor Pearl Holdings Limited (HKEX-listed), with subsidiaries including Blue Estuary Asset Management (HK) Limited.
On 28 May 2026 at 22:15 HKT, the Chief Information Officer, Dr. Kelvin Yip, reported unauthorised access to the order management server hosted at DeltaVoyage Data Centres Limited, causing a 6-hour outage in the Hong Kong retail division.
Preliminary assessment indicates approximately HK$42–55 million in revenue will be deferred into Q3 and 1H EBITDA may be reduced by 5–7%; relative to the Group’s c. HK$5.2 billion market capitalisation, this is likely price-sensitive.
The incident also affected the virtual data room for the proposed acquisition of Eastward Marine Logistics Limited, a notifiable transaction not yet announced under Chapter 14 of the Listing Rules and subject to confidentiality.
Names and work emails of 34 Hong Kong employees and contact details of 12 fund clients may have been accessed; no HKID numbers, bank accounts, or passwords were stored, and passwords have since been reset.
The specific issuer impact and transaction status described above have not been released on HKEXnews, and no selective disclosure to analysts or investors is permitted.
For clarity, the 2025 Sustainability Report filed via HKEXnews on 17 April 2026 discussed legacy cybersecurity controls and does not constitute disclosure of this incident.
Media reports on 29 May 2026 about a regional service disruption at DeltaVoyage were broad and non-issuer-specific; such public information should not be taken as disclosing our internal financial impact.
Subject to drafting and board clearance, we plan to submit an inside information announcement through the HKEX e-Submission System before market open on 3 June 2026 and will request a trading halt if not cleared by 07:30.
During the blackout period ahead of interim results under the Model Code, directors and relevant employees must suspend dealings in the Company’s securities until publication of the announcement.
Ms. Ada Law, Chief Compliance Officer, has informed the chair and Audit Committee and is liaising with the SFC as necessary regarding compliance with the inside information provisions.
